lessons.title: Orchestra Map by Instrument Family
lessons.description: There can be more than a hundred players in an orchestra, but they all belong to four different families of instruments: strings, woodwinds, brass, and percussion.

lessons.title: Orchestra Map by Instrument
lessons.description: Each family of instruments is distinguished by unique characteristics, such as the materials used to create the instruments and the different ways they produce sound. Each type of instrument is also unique.

lessons.title: String Instruments
lessons.description:
Except for the harp, string instruments in an orchestra are pear shaped, made of wood, and hollow inside. They are mostly played with a bow, a stick with horse hair. When a player draws the bow across the string, it produces friction and creates sound.

In an orchestra, strings are usually: violins, violas, cellos, basses, and harp. Occasionally, in special circumstances, there are other string instruments on stage. When something atypical is scheduled in a concert, the program will tell you all about it.

lessons.title: Woodwinds
lessons.description: Woodwind instruments produce sound when air is blown through or across an opening. Common woodwinds in an orchestra include flute, clarinet, oboe, and bassoon.

lessons.title: Brass Instruments
lessons.description: Brass instruments create sound through buzzing lips into a mouthpiece. Common brass instruments in an orchestra include trumpet, trombone, French horn, and tuba.

lessons.title: Percussion Instruments
lessons.description: Percussion instruments make sound when struck, shaken, or scraped. In an orchestra, percussion can include timpani, snare drum, bass drum, cymbals, triangle, and many others.

questions.title: What instrument is playing?
questions.subtitle: On the right, select the orchestral instrument you hear in the sound clip below.

questions.title: What instrument family is playing?
questions.subtitle: Listen to the clip and choose which instrument family you hear.

transitions.title: Welcome to Carnegie Hall!
transitions.text: You're about to attend a concert at Carnegie Hall. There will be an orchestra on stage. This is your crash course to orchestral instruments, where they are usually located on stage, and each of their sounds.

transitions.title: Now let’s listen more closely.
transitions.text: In the next section, you will listen to each instrument family play. Each listening section introduces a specific family: Strings, Woodwinds, Brass, and Percussion.

transitions.title:
Part I:
Are You Ready to Identify Instruments?
transitions.text: You’ve learned where instruments sit and what they sound like. Now it’s time for a short quiz.
